title: tephrosia lepida subsp. nigrescens
intro: Tephrosia lepida subsp. nigrescens, also known as Black Wild Bean or Black Horse Bean, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Tephrosia lepida subsp. nigrescens include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions.
